metadata.title: Detox — Understand smartphone addiction
metadata.description: An interactive video experience and a scientifically validated questionnaire to help you understand your relationship with your smartphone.

hero.title: Understand your relationship with your smartphone
hero.subtitle: An interactive video experience and a scientifically validated questionnaire, designed with and for Swiss schools.

pillars.title: Seven pillars, one coherent approach
pillars.subtitle: Every project decision ties back to at least one of these axes.

durable.title: Durable
durable.description: GDPR compliance, maintainable code, open-source questionnaire.

addiction.title: Addiction
addiction.description: Solid scientific grounding (SAS-SV scale), responsible narrative.

collaboration.title: Collaboration
collaboration.description: Dashboard for teachers, class-level aggregated data.

interdisciplinaire.title: Interdisciplinary
interdisciplinaire.description: Psychology, UX, public health and data science combined.

accessible.title: Accessible
accessible.description: WCAG 2.1 AA, keyboard navigation, screen readers.

footer.tagline: Detox — an HEFP project for youth digital health.
footer.gdpr: Your answers are anonymous. See our {policy}.
footer.rights: © 2026 Detox project. Open-source parts under MIT licence.

consent.title: Before you start
consent.body: To improve the experience, we record 100% anonymous data (answers, video interactions). No personal information is collected. Your data is never sold.

lines.3: The phone buzzes once, then again.
lines.4: Three new messages. An Insta notification. A live on TikTok.

options.B: I flip it face-down and start studying.

lines.2: Video, video, video. A friend in a story.
lines.3: You reply quickly. You scroll some more.
lines.4: 10:13 PM. You put the phone down. The math test is in eleven hours.

lines.1: 8:47 PM. The phone is face-down on the desk.
lines.3: A notification flashes. You hesitate for a second.
lines.4: 9:35 PM. You close the book. You feel a little tired — but ready.

choice_2.prompt: Morning, at the bus stop. Your friends show you a meme. The phone buzzes. How do you want to spend the next twenty minutes?

options.A: I pull out my phone too — it's easier.
options.B: I leave it in my pocket and listen.

lines.2: The three of you laugh at the same video.
lines.3: No-one is really looking at each other.
lines.4: The bus arrives. You didn't hear what Léa was saying.

lines.1: The phone buzzes. You feel it.
lines.2: Léa tells you about an argument with her sister.
lines.3: You ask a question. She answers at length.
lines.4: The bus arrives. You know things about her life you didn't know yesterday.

lines.1: None of these stories is "the right one".
lines.2: But each small choice shapes your relationship with the phone.
lines.3: We'll ask you ten questions to better understand yours.

closing.title: And you?
closing.body: None of these stories is "the right one". Each small choice shapes your relationship with the phone. We'll ask you ten questions to better understand yours.

survey.title: SAS-SV questionnaire
survey.subtitle: 10 statements about your smartphone use. Pick the number that fits you best. There are no right or wrong answers — be honest with yourself.
survey.intro: For each statement: 1 = strongly disagree, 6 = strongly agree. Your answers are anonymous and saved as you go — you can close the page and resume later.

results.title: Your results
results.instrumentNote: Pre-validated French adaptation of the SAS-SV (Kwon et al., 2013). Independent validation in progress — see methodology.

low.heading: Moderate use
low.body: Your score suggests your smartphone has a measured place in your day. Keep noticing how you feel when you put it down.
low.reflect: A question for you: what do you enjoy most about moments without a smartphone?

moderate.heading: Use to watch
moderate.body: Your score is in an intermediate zone. Several habits deserve attention — no alarm, but stay mindful.
moderate.reflect: A question for you: if you had to cut your screen time by one hour a day, what would you start with?

high.heading: Heavy use
high.body: Your score indicates heavy smartphone use, with possible effects on focus, sleep or relationships. This doesn't define who you are — it's a signal to open a conversation.
high.reflect: A question for you: who that you trust could you talk to this week about your smartphone use?

symptoms.title: Breakdown by dimension
symptoms.subtitle: Each bar reflects a different aspect of your smartphone relationship.

resources.title: Support resources
resources.subtitle: If you want to talk, these Swiss services are free and confidential.

proJuventute.name: Pro Juventute · 147
proJuventute.description: 24/7 advice by phone, chat or SMS, free and anonymous.

promotionSante.name: Promotion Santé Suisse
promotionSante.description: Public foundation for prevention and mental health.

stopAddiction.name: Addiction Suisse
stopAddiction.description: Information and support around addictions, including digital ones.

results.disclaimer: This questionnaire is a personal reflection tool, not a medical diagnosis. If you are concerned, consult a healthcare professional.

errors.generic: Something went wrong. Please try again.
